name: tech-debt-audit
tagline: Read-only, tool-driven skill that audits a repo across 9 tech-debt dimensions and writes a cited TECH_DEBT_AUDIT.md report.
verdict: Low-risk audit skill: it only reads (grep/glob/bash/lsp) and writes a single local Markdown report; secret-pattern greps are for reporting, not exfiltration, and no destructive or hidden actions were found. Minor issues: claims to be language-agnostic but many concrete commands hardcode TypeScript ast-grep patterns, no negative-trigger or non-JS fallback gui

highlights.summary: Recommends an optional 'CodeGraph' MCP server from a single unverified GitHub repo (github.com/colbymchenry/codegraph, line 13) as an enhancement, with no vetting guidance for users who choose to inst
No explicit 'do NOT use when' guidance is given anywhere in the description or body, so users can't easily tell when this heavyweight audit is the wrong tool (e.g., quick single-file review).

Claims to be language-agnostic ('map the language stack' via glob for .ts/.py/etc, line 38) but most concrete Phase-1 commands hardcode TypeScript-specific ast-grep patterns (`-l ts .`, e.g. lines 65-
